nodes.name: 🔔 Webhook — Generate PDF (called by WF02B)
nodes.notes: Called by WF02B after product content is generated. Receives full product data including content, layout preferences, and brand config. Returns: pdf_url, pdf_filename, page_count.

values.content: You are a professional PDF planner designer specializing in creating print-ready productivity planners. You generate complete, valid HTML that renders beautifully when converted to PDF via wkhtmltopdf. Your HTML uses only CSS that wkhtmltopdf supports (no flexbox gap, no CSS grid, no modern features — use table-based layout or basic floats/positioning). Every page must have exactly the right dimensions for A4 printing. Your planners are genuinely useful — every field serves a purpose. Brand voice: ClearStack Studio. Tone: professional, clean, minimal. Respond in valid JSON only.

=Generate complete HTML for a {{ $json.product.type }} PDF planner.

PRODUCT DETAILS:
Title: {{ $json.product.title }}
Description: {{ $json.product.description }}
Brand: {{ $json.product.brand }}
Tagline: {{ $json.product.tagline }}
Website: {{ $json.product.website }}

BRAND COLORS:
Primary: {{ $json.product.primary_color }} (navy)
Accent: {{ $json.product.accent_color }} (gold)
Text: {{ $json.product.text_color }}

LAYOUT PAGES NEEDED: {{ $json.layout.pages.join(', ') }}
Orientation: {{ $json.layout.orientation }}
Size: {{ $json.layout.size }}

WKHTMLTOPDF CONSTRAINTS (CRITICAL):
- A4 portrait: 210mm × 297mm (794px × 1123px at 96dpi)
- Use @page CSS rule for page dimensions
- No flexbox gap property (use margin instead)
- No CSS grid (use table or float)
- No SVG in CSS backgrounds
- Font: use Google Fonts @import or system fonts only
- Page breaks: use page-break-after: always
- Tables for aligned layouts
- Print-safe colors only (no rgba with low opacity)

CONTENT REQUIREMENTS:
- Cover page: product title, brand name, tagline, decorative elements using CSS only
- Welcome page: brief instructions on how to use the planner (3-5 sentences)
- Main pages: functional, printable layout with appropriate fields
  Weekly planner pages need: day columns, time slots, priorities section, notes
  Monthly pages need: calendar grid, goals, notes
  Goal tracker pages need: goal, why, milestones, deadline, progress bar placeholder
- Every page: small brand name + website in footer
- Page numbers in footer

DESIGN PRINCIPLES:
- Clean, minimal, professional
- Generous white space for handwriting
- Clear visual hierarchy
- Print-friendly (no dark backgrounds on content pages)
- Accent color used sparingly for headers and dividers only

Respond ONLY with this JSON:
{
  "html": "Complete HTML document from <!DOCTYPE html> to </html>. All CSS inline in <style> tag. No external dependencies except Google Fonts @import.",
  "page_count": 67,
  "pages_breakdown": ["Cover", "Welcome", "52 Weekly pages", "12 Monthly pages", "5 Notes pages"],
  "design_notes": "Brief description of design choices"
}

nodes.name: 🧠 llama3 — Generate Planner HTML
nodes.notes: llama3 generates complete HTML for the planner. Temperature 0.5 for consistent, clean output. Full 8k context window — planner HTML can be large. Processing time: 8-20 min on CPU VPS. This is the core of the product creation pipeline.

nodes.name: ⚙️ Parse + Fix HTML for wkhtmltopdf
nodes.notes: Parses llama3 output, fixes common wkhtmltopdf incompatibilities (flexbox gap, missing @page rule, charset). Validates HTML length before proceeding.

parameters.command:
=# Create output directory
mkdir -p /tmp/pdf_gen

# Write HTML to temp file
cat > '{{ $json.html_path }}' << 'HTMLEOF'
{{ $json.html_content }}
HMLEOF

# Verify HTML was written
if [ ! -f '{{ $json.html_path }}' ]; then
  echo 'ERROR: HTML file not created'
  exit 1
fi

html_size=$(wc -c < '{{ $json.html_path }}')
echo "HTML file size: ${html_size} bytes"

if [ "$html_size" -lt 1000 ]; then
  echo 'ERROR: HTML file too small, generation likely failed'
  exit 1
fi

echo "HTML written successfully: {{ $json.html_path }}"

nodes.name: 💾 Write HTML to VPS
nodes.notes: Writes the HTML to a temp file on the VPS. Uses heredoc to handle special characters safely.

parameters.command:
=# Convert HTML to PDF using wkhtmltopdf
# Install if not present: apt-get install -y wkhtmltopdf

ORIENTATION="{{ $('⚙️ Prepare PDF Config').first().json.layout.orientation }}"
HTML_FILE="{{ $('⚙️ Parse + Fix HTML for wkhtmltopdf').first().json.html_path }}"
PDF_FILE="{{ $('⚙️ Parse + Fix HTML for wkhtmltopdf').first().json.output_path }}"
PRODUCT_TITLE="{{ $('⚙️ Prepare PDF Config').first().json.product.title }}"

echo "Converting: $HTML_FILE → $PDF_FILE"
echo "Orientation: $ORIENTATION"

# wkhtmltopdf with print-optimized settings
wkhtmltopdf \
  --orientation "$ORIENTATION" \
  --page-size A4 \
  --margin-top 0 \
  --margin-right 0 \
  --margin-bottom 0 \
  --margin-left 0 \
  --encoding UTF-8 \
  --enable-local-file-access \
  --javascript-delay 500 \
  --no-background \
  --print-media-type \
  --dpi 300 \
  --image-quality 100 \
  --title "$PRODUCT_TITLE" \
  "$HTML_FILE" \
  "$PDF_FILE" 2>&1

# Check result
if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
  echo 'ERROR: wkhtmltopdf conversion failed'
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -f "$PDF_FILE" ]; then
  echo 'ERROR: PDF file not created'
  exit 1
fi

PDF_SIZE=$(wc -c < "$PDF_FILE")
echo "PDF created successfully: $PDF_FILE"
echo "PDF size: ${PDF_SIZE} bytes"

# Get page count
PAGE_COUNT=$(pdfinfo "$PDF_FILE" 2>/dev/null | grep Pages | awk '{print $2}' || echo 'unknown')
echo "Page count: $PAGE_COUNT"

nodes.name: ⚙️ Convert HTML → PDF (wkhtmltopdf)
nodes.notes: Converts HTML to PDF at 300 DPI for print quality. --no-background prevents dark CSS backgrounds from printing. --print-media-type uses @media print CSS rules. Outputs to /tmp/pdf_gen/. Processing time: 10-60 seconds depending on page count.

parameters.command:
=# Upload PDF to Cloudflare R2 (or S3-compatible storage)
# Install rclone if not present: curl https://rclone.org/install.sh | sudo bash
# Configure: rclone config → add S3/R2 remote named 'r2'

PDF_FILE="{{ $('⚙️ Parse + Fix HTML for wkhtmltopdf').first().json.output_path }}"
FILENAME="{{ $('⚙️ Prepare PDF Config').first().json.filename }}"
PRODUCT_ID="{{ $('⚙️ Prepare PDF Config').first().json.product.id }}"

# Upload to R2/S3 bucket
rclone copy \
  "$PDF_FILE" \
  "r2:clearstack-products/pdfs/" \
  --s3-acl public-read 2>&1

if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
  echo 'ERROR: Upload failed'
  # Fallback: serve from local nginx
  mkdir -p /var/www/clearstack-downloads
  cp "$PDF_FILE" "/var/www/clearstack-downloads/$FILENAME"
  echo "FALLBACK_URL=https://clearstackstudio.com/downloads/$FILENAME"
  exit 0
fi

# Public URL (Cloudflare R2 public bucket URL)
PUBLIC_URL="https://pub-YOUR_R2_ACCOUNT_ID.r2.dev/pdfs/$FILENAME"
echo "UPLOAD_SUCCESS=true"
echo "PUBLIC_URL=$PUBLIC_URL"
echo "PRODUCT_ID=$PRODUCT_ID"
echo "FILENAME=$FILENAME"

# Clean up HTML temp file
rm -f "{{ $('⚙️ Parse + Fix HTML for wkhtmltopdf').first().json.html_path }}"
echo 'HTML temp file cleaned up'

nodes.name: ☁️ Upload PDF to R2/S3
nodes.notes: SETUP: (1) Create Cloudflare R2 bucket 'clearstack-products' — free 10GB. (2) Configure rclone with R2 credentials. (3) Replace YOUR_R2_ACCOUNT_ID with your actual Cloudflare account ID. Fallback: serves from local nginx if R2 fails.

nodes.name: 🔌 Trigger WF13 — Generate Mockups
nodes.notes: Passes PDF data to WF13 for mockup generation. WF13 creates the Etsy listing images. After WF13 completes, it triggers WF03 for publishing.

meta.description: WF02C: PDF Generation Pipeline. Receives product data from WF02B. llama3 generates complete planner HTML. wkhtmltopdf converts to print-quality PDF at 300 DPI. Uploads to Cloudflare R2 (free 10GB). Returns public PDF URL. Triggers WF13 for mockup generation. Supports 7 planner types: weekly, daily, goal tracker, habit tracker, client tracker, content calendar, budget planner.
vps_setup_required: rclone config: add remote named 'r2' with Cloudflare R2 credentials
